THE REALITY

Inefficient workflows are slowing down clinical care.

THE QUESTION

WHAT IS PRODUCTIVITY INTELLIGENCE (PI)?

DEFINITION 01

PI is the intersection of smart technology and peak productivity.

DEFINITION 02

PI identifies bottlenecks, eliminates waste, and allows hospitals to build, compete, and share solutions.

THE ANSWER

The best place to find these solutions?

21 July 2026 · Dewan Produktiviti, MPC, MATRADE · Kuala Lumpur

Build the Future of Healthcare from Within.

Welcome to the first ever Productivity Intelligence Inno-vathon. We are empowering healthcare staff to eliminate inefficiencies by building agile, low-cost, in-house digital solutions that deliver immediate wins.

HTPN LEGACY
WHY THIS CONVENTION

MORE THAN A CONVENTION.
MALAYSIA'S LIGHTHOUSE
HOSPITAL IN ACTION.

HTPN's Healthcare Intelligence Office has built over 50 live clinical applications entirely in-house — without external vendors, without high-cost licences. Led by practicing clinicians, the HIO has transformed how a 135-year-old public hospital builds digital solutions.

This convention shares that model — openly, freely — with every KKM hospital in Malaysia. Come to learn, compete, and take the insourcing revolution home.

  • Insourcing model — built by clinicians, for clinicians
  • Free entry for all KKM staff & healthcare professionals
  • National-level recognition through MPC case study documentation
  • Reward culture — celebrate innovation on a national stage
FEATURED SPEAKERS

LEARN FROM THOSE
BUILDING MALAYSIA'S
HEALTHCARE AI FUTURE

From AI ethics pioneers to hardware deployment experts to reward culture champions — Innova-thon brings UPM and UiTM's leading researchers to the national stage. Expect practical insights, clinical depth, and talks that move beyond theory.

OUR JURY

Convention Day · 4:30 PM
PHASE ONE
Registration & Proposal Submission
May 2026 · 4 Weeks

Google Form submission, application demonstration video, and a 1-page project summary outlining your innovation.

PHASE TWO
Judges' Pre-Assessment
June 2026 · 2 Weeks

Evaluation of demonstration videos by the judging panel using a standardised rubric. Written feedback provided to all participants.

PHASE THREE
Mentorship & Improvement Session
June – Early July 2026 · 3 Weeks

Guided support by HIO HTPN via online sessions — covering app development, enhancement, deployment, and data training.

PHASE FOUR
Final Day — Oral Presentation
Mid-July 2026

An 8-minute live presentation followed by a 4-minute Q&A session across five themed breakout rooms.

PHASE FIVE
Announcement of Winners
Convention Day · 4:30 PM

Award presentation and recognition ceremony during the closing session of Innova-thon 2026.

MARKING RUBRIC

Every project is evaluated against eight standardised criteria.

01—  Clear Problem Statement Addresses a real clinical or operational gap with a well-defined, justified solution.
02—  Creativity Original idea that stands apart from existing solutions in the field.
03—  User Friendly Intuitive interface adoptable by end-users without complex training.
04—  Deployment Technically stable and integrates with existing hospital systems.
05—  Data Validation AI model is accurate, data-secure, and privacy-compliant.
06—  Clear Output & Outcome Delivers measurable results — time saved, accuracy gained, costs reduced.
07—  Scalability Can be adopted beyond the originating hospital or team.
08—  Sustainability Cost-effective and operable without reliance on external vendors.

PROGRAMME SCHEDULE

FULL DAY PROGRAMME
21 JULY 2026

Dewan Produktiviti, MPC, MATRADE, Kuala Lumpur · 8:00am – 5:00pm

08:00 AM Registration & Event Pass Collection ▼

Delegates collect event passes, goodie bags and lanyards at the registration counter. Exhibition zone opens for early browsing.

09:00 AM PLENARY 1Prof. Madya Dr Mohd Rafee bin BaharudinAI Transformation in Healthcare: Roadmap, Strategy & Vision – 2030 Prediction ▼
AI Transformation in Healthcare: Roadmap, Strategy & Vision – 2030 Prediction

An authoritative overview of the current AI landscape across Malaysia's public health ecosystem — from clinical decision support to population health analytics. Prof Rafee draws on his WHO-funded research to map where AI is already reshaping patient outcomes, where the critical gaps remain, and what ethical and data governance frameworks must underpin every deployment. A roadmap for what Malaysia's healthcare future can and must look like.

Faculty of Medicine & Health Sciences · Universiti Putra Malaysia
09:30 AM PLENARY 2Prof. Madya Dr Shukor SanimGrant Opportunities, Networking & Potential University Collaboration in AI Healthcare ▼
Grant Opportunities, Networking & Potential University Collaboration in AI Healthcare

A practical masterclass in navigating Malaysia's research funding landscape for AI-driven healthcare projects. Prof Shukor demystifies the grant application process — from Ministry of Higher Education funding to international research collaborations — and reveals what review panels are really looking for. He also covers hardware investment realities (GPU infrastructure, cloud vs on-premise), AI tool selection frameworks, and how clinical teams can position their work for academic co-authorship and sustained institutional support.

Faculty of Computer & Mathematical Sciences · UiTM Perlis
10:00 AM PLENARY 3Dr Ahmad Hakiim JamaluddinAI Transformation of Reward Culture & Quick-Win Projects ▼
AI Transformation of Reward Culture & Quick-Win Projects – Experience in Participating and Organising International AI Competitions

Why do some hospital teams build extraordinary AI solutions while others stall? Dr Hakiim presents a compelling evidence-based framework for engineering an innovation culture from the ground up — using structured reward systems, well-designed AI competitions, and recognition pathways that make clinicians feel their contributions matter at a national level. Drawing from his experience running large-scale AI challenges, he covers competition design, rubric construction, AI model evaluation methodology, and how to sustain innovation momentum long after the competition ends.

Department of Mathematics & Statistics · Universiti Putra Malaysia
10:30 AM Breakout Sessions — Innova-thon Competition ▼

5 parallel breakout rooms running simultaneously · 8 min presentation + 4 min Q&A per project

ROOM 1
Patient Journey
/ Customer Experience
ROOM 2
Clinician Journey
ROOM 3
Clinical Interactive Dashboard
ROOM 4
Predictive Modelling
(Disease-Based)
ROOM 5
Management Productivity
01:00 PM Lunch, Solat Zohor & Innovation Showcase ▼

Buffet lunch for 200 delegates (calorie-tagged). Solat Zohor break. Innovation Showcase exhibition zone fully open — delegates explore all 50+ AI applications and vendor booths.

02:00 PM Continuation — Breakout Sessions & Live Demos ▼

Breakout sessions resume across all 5 rooms. Live demonstrations of HIO HTPN flagship applications including EBL Estimator AI, TherapyAI, Clinical Document Checker, and Dashboard ED.

02:30 PM Arrival of VIPs & Special Guests ▼

Arrival and reception of VIPs and special guests. Ushering to reserved seats.

03:00 PM PLENARY 4Dr Siv Azhar Merican Bin AbdullahAI-Driven Organisational Change Management at HTPN ▼
AI-Driven Organisational Change Management and Leadership Transformation at HTPN – Building a Digital Mindset
Director, Hospital Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin (HTPN), Kajang
Speech by Director General, MPC
Speech by Director General, Ministry of Health Malaysia (MOH)
Closing Ceremony, Official Launch Gimmick & Video Montage

Prize-Giving Ceremony for Competition Winners

● Gold — RM 500
● Silver — RM 300
● Bronze — RM 200
● 5× Consolation — RM 100

Certificate presentation for all participants (co-signed by Pengarah HTPN & MPC). Cenderahati for VIPs, judges and speakers.

05:00 PM End of Programme / Adjournment ▼

Official close. Group photo session. Networking and follow-up conversations. Delegates may continue exploring the Innovation Showcase.

BEFORE YOU ARRIVE IN JULY

HOW TO GET THERE
Dewan Produktiviti, MPC, MATRADE, Jalan Khidmat Usaha, Off Jalan Duta. KLCC LRT — 5 min walk. Free parking on-site for delegates.
GET DIRECTIONS →
WHAT TO BRING
Laptop + charger. App demo ready and tested. Event pass (email confirmation). Hospital ID badge. Competitor? Bring printed rubric.
DOWNLOAD CHECKLIST →
STAY NEARBY
Hotels near MATRADE from RM120/night. Conference partner rate available soon. We recommend booking early — 21 July fills fast.
